How to Apply for Sarkari Job Online 2026

  1. Find the right Sarkari Job: Use the vacancy table or sector links above on OnlyJobs. Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, or Engineering. Check the last date and eligibility carefully.

  2. Read the official notification: Click on the job listing to open the official notification PDF. Read all eligibility conditions — age limit, educational qualification, experience, category relaxations.

  3. Visit the official recruitment website: Never apply through third-party sites. Use the direct official link provided on OnlyJobs. Look for "Apply Online" or "Register" on the official portal.

  4. Register / Create account: Sign up using your valid email ID and active mobile number. Keep your Aadhaar, bank account details, and email accessible.

  5. Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualifications, category (General/OBC/SC/ST/EWS), address, and other required information accurately.

  6. Upload documents: Scan and upload recent passport-size photograph, signature, 10th certificate (for DOB proof), educational certificates, caste certificate (if applicable). Check file size and format (JPG/PDF as specified).

  7. Pay application fee: Pay via Net Banking, UPI, Debit/Credit Card as applicable. Some categories (SC/ST/PwD/Women) may be exempted from fee. Keep the payment receipt.

  8. Submit and save confirmation: Review all details before final submission. After submitting, download the application printout / PDF confirmation with your registration number. Store it safely for admit card, exam, and future reference.

Why Sarkari Job? Benefits of Government Employment in India

Job Security
Permanent employment — cannot be terminated without due process. Lifelong career stability.
7th Pay Commission Salary
Fixed salary + DA, HRA, TA allowances. DA revised every 6 months. Regular pay revisions.
Medical Benefits
Free or subsidised medical treatment for employee and family under CGHS / state health schemes.
Housing Benefits
Government quarters (where available) or HRA (8–27% of basic pay). No private rent worries.
Pension (NPS / OPS)
Post-retirement monthly pension under NPS or OPS (state-specific). Financial security after service.
Work-Life Balance
Fixed working hours. Paid casual, medical, earned leave. National and state holidays. No overtime culture.
Career Growth
Regular promotions through DPC, ACR-based upgrades, MACP benefits (3 financial upgrades guaranteed).
Children's Education Allowance
Reimbursement of children's school fees (₹2,812/month per child up to 2 children, revised periodically).

Frequently Asked Questions – Sarkari Job 2026

What is Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी)?

Sarkari Job (सरकारी नौकरी) refers to government employment in India — jobs offered by central government departments, state governments, public sector undertakings (PSUs), banks, railways, defence forces, and other government-funded organisations. These jobs offer permanent employment, fixed salary as per 7th Pay Commission, pension, medical benefits, and unmatched job security.

How to find Sarkari Job vacancy 2026 online?

To find online Sarkari Job vacancies in 2026: (1) Visit OnlyJobs daily for updated notifications. (2) Filter by your qualification — 10th, 12th, Graduate, PG. (3) Filter by sector — Railway, Banking, SSC, Defence, State PSC, Teaching. (4) Check last dates carefully. (5) Apply through the official department website using the direct link provided. Enable push notifications on OnlyJobs for instant alerts.

What Sarkari Job can I get after 10th pass?

After 10th pass, you can apply for: Railway Group D (RRB), Indian Army Soldier posts, Postal Mail Guard, SSC MTS / Havaldar, Constable GD, Anganwadi Worker/Helper, ITI Apprentice in PSUs (IOCL, HPCL, NTPC), MCL / Coal India posts, and various state government peon/chowkidar posts. Thousands of 10th pass Sarkari Job vacancies open every year.

What is the salary in Sarkari Job 2026?

Sarkari Job salary in 2026 follows the 7th Pay Commission: Group D / MTS: ₹22,000–₹26,000/month. Clerk / Group C: ₹32,000–₹48,000/month. Officer Grade (SSC CGL, Bank PO): ₹47,000–₹85,000/month. IAS/IPS (UPSC): ₹56,100–₹1,44,200 basic pay + DA, HRA, TA, medical and other allowances. State government salaries vary by state pay commission.

Which Bharat Sarkari Job has the most vacancies in 2026?

The largest Bharat Sarkari Job recruitments in 2026 include: Indian Army Agniveer (40,000+ posts), RRB Group D (22,000+ posts), SSC CGL (14,582 posts), SSC MTS (10,000+ posts), UP Police Constable (multiple), Bihar Police (multiple), IBPS Clerk (8,000+ posts), and various state PSC recruitments. Total active sarkari vacancies in India exceed 52,920 as of March 2026.

What is the best website to find Sarkari Job in India?

OnlyJobs is India's most trusted portal for Sarkari Job notifications since 2011, serving over 10 million users. Official direct sources include: ssc.nic.in (SSC), indianrailways.gov.in (Railways), upsc.gov.in (UPSC), ibps.in (Banking), and respective state PSC websites. OnlyJobs aggregates all official notifications in one place with direct apply links.

How to apply for Sarkari Job online in 2026?

To apply for Sarkari Job online: (1) Read official notification PDF. (2) Check eligibility. (3) Visit official recruitment website. (4) Register with email/mobile. (5) Fill application form. (6) Upload photo, signature, and certificates. (7) Pay fee via Net Banking/UPI/Debit card. (8) Submit and lication confirmation. Save the registration number for admit card and future reference.

What are the benefits of Sarkari Job in India?

Key benefits: (1) Lifelong job security. (2) 7th Pay Commission salary + DA revised biannually. (3) HRA (8–27% of basic). (4) Medical benefits (CGHS). (5) Pension under NPS/OPS. (6) Paid leave — casual, earned, medical. (7) Gratuity after 5 years. (8) Children's education allowance. (9) Fixed working hours and work-life balance. (10) Prestige and social recognition in Indian society.

What is the age limit for Sarkari Job?

Age limits vary by post: Group D (18–25 years), SSC CGL (18–32 years), Bank PO (20–30 years), UPSC Civil Services (21–32 years), Defence Agniveer (17.5–23 years). Age relaxations: SC/ST — 5 years, OBC — 3 years, PwD — 10 years, Ex-Servicemen — 3 years (general), Government servants — up to 5 years. Always verify in the official notification for each post.

What is Bharat Sarkari Job?

Bharat Sarkari Job means government job opportunities across the entire country of India (Bharat) — spanning central and state government vacancies across all 28 states and 8 union territories. The term emphasises pan-India coverage. OnlyJobs covers all Bharat Sarkari Job notifications from UPSC, SSC, Railways, Banking, Defence, state PSCs, and all other government departments.

About this page: Sarkari Job 2026 information on this page is compiled by the OnlyJobs editorial team from official government recruitment notifications. All vacancy data is verified against official websites before listing. OnlyJobs has been India's trusted government job portal since 2011, serving 10+ million users annually. For any recruitment, always refer to the official notification linked above.

About OnlyJobs 2026

OnlyJobs (Government Jobs) are highly sought-after employment opportunities in India. These positions offer job security, good salary packages, allowances, and retirement benefits. Our platform provides comprehensive information about all types of government vacancies including:

  • Central Government Jobs: UPSC, SSC, Railway, Banking, Defence, PSUs
  • State Government Jobs: State PSCs, Police, Teachers, Healthcare workers
  • Qualification-based Jobs: 10th Pass, 12th Pass, Graduate, Post Graduate, Engineering
  • Category-wise Jobs: General, OBC, SC, ST, EWS, Ex-Servicemen, PwD

Frequently Asked Questions - OnlyJobs 2026

Q1: What is the eligibility for OnlyJobs?

Eligibility varies by position. Generally, candidates need to be Indian citizens aged 18-35 years (with age relaxation for reserved categories). Educational qualifications range from 10th pass to post-graduation depending on the job.

Q2: How to prepare for Government Job exams?

Preparation includes understanding the exam pattern, studying from standard books, practicing previous year papers, taking mock tests, and staying updated with current affairs. Focus on subjects like General Knowledge, Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, and English.

Q3: Which are the best Government Jobs in 2026?

Top government jobs include IAS/IPS (UPSC), Bank PO, SSC CGL, Railway Group A & B, Defence Services, PSU Jobs, State PCS, and Teaching positions. These offer excellent career growth, job security, and benefits.

Q4: What is the application process for OnlyJobs?

The process typically involves: 1) Online registration on the official website, 2) Filling application form, 3) Uploading documents (photo, signature, certificates), 4) Payment of application fee, 5) Downloading admit card, 6) Appearing for examination, 7) Document verification if selected.

Q5: How often are new OnlyJobs notifications released?

New government job notifications are released daily. Major recruitments like UPSC, SSC, IBPS have annual calendars, while state governments and PSUs release notifications throughout the year. We update our portal daily with 647+ active job listings.

WCD Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Apply Online wcd.nic.in

WCD Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – Apply online for the latest Anganwadi Worker, Anganwadi Helper and Supervisor vacancies through the official WCD/ICDS portals such as wcd.nic.in and state ICDS portals. Candidates can check state-wise and district-wise Anganwadi vacancy lists, eligibility, qualifications, age limit, salary, and online application dates, and download the Anganwadi Application Form 2026. Start your career in Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) and stay updated with the latest Anganwadi job notifications from all major states including Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Telangana, Uttar Pradesh, Andhra Pradesh, Maharashtra, Bihar and more.

Anganwadi Worker Salary / Honorarium 2026

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers receive a monthly honorarium (not a salary), which includes a Central Government component and an additional State Government component. The table below shows the total honorarium per month for key states in 2026.

Selection Process for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026

There is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Helper posts. Selection is merit-based on academic qualifications. For Supervisor and CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted.

How to Apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Online

  • Visit the official portal: Go to your state's official WCD / ICDS website or the direct link provided in the vacancy table above. For central information, visit wcd.nic.in.
  • Find the recruitment notification: Click on the "Recruitment" or "Anganwadi Recruitment 2026" section.
  • Download and read the notification: Download and read the official notification PDF carefully before applying.
  • Register / Login: Create a new account using your mobile number and Aadhaar. If already registered, log in with your credentials.
  • Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualification, address, and select your preferred district/ward. Ensure you apply only for a ward where you are a permanent resident.
  • Upload documents: Upload scanned copies of your passport-size photograph, signature, 10th/12th marksheet, Aadhaar card, and any caste/domicile certificate if required.
  • Review and submit: Check all entries carefully before final submission. Download and print the application confirmation / acknowledgement slip for your records.

Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – FAQ's

How to apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 online?

Visit your state's official WCD / ICDS portal (link provided in table above). Register with mobile & Aadhaar, fill the application form, upload documents (photo, 10th certificate, Aadhaar, domicile), and submit before the last date. Print the application acknowledgement slip after submission.

What is the eligibility for Anganwadi Worker 2026?

Candidates must have passed 10th or 12th standard from a recognized board. Age limit is 18–35 years (varies by state). Must be a permanent resident of the ward/village for which vacancy exists. Only female candidates are eligible for Worker and Helper posts.

What is the salary of Anganwadi Worker in 2026?

Anganwadi Worker receives a monthly honorarium of ₹10,000–₹20,000 depending on the state (Karnataka and TN are highest at ~₹14,000). Anganwadi Helper gets ₹5,000–₹7,000/month. The Central Government pays ₹4,500/month as base honorarium, and states add their own component on top.

Is there a written exam for Anganwadi jobs?

No, there is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Anganwadi Helper posts. Selection is purely merit-based on academic qualification marks (10th / 12th percentage). For Supervisor / CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted depending on the state.

What is the age limit for Anganwadi Helper?

Age limit is 18 to 35 years as of 1st January 2026 in most states. SC/ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (maximum age 40). OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (maximum age 38). PwD candidates get additional relaxation as per state rules.

What documents are required for Anganwadi job application?

Required documents: Aadhaar card, 10th/12th marksheet and certificate, domicile/residence certificate, caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC), passport-size photograph, scanned signature, and EWS/PwD certificate if applicable. Graduation certificate is needed for Supervisor post applications.

What is ICDS recruitment 2026?

ICDS stands for Integrated Child Development Services - the government programme under which Anganwadi centres operate nationwide. ICDS recruitment 2026 refers to hiring of Anganwadi Workers, Helpers, Supervisors, and CDPOs by state WCD departments under the Ministry of Women & Child Development (wcd.nic.in).

Is Anganwadi Worker a permanent government job?

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers are honorary workers, not regular government employees. They receive a fixed monthly honorarium (not salary) and are entitled to benefits like ESI, health insurance, and maternity benefits. Supervisors and CDPOs are regular government employees in most states and receive full pay & grade pay.

Can I apply for Anganwadi job from a different district?

No. Anganwadi Workers and Helpers must be permanent residents of the same ward/village/district where the vacancy exists. A domicile or residence certificate is mandatory. Applications from outside the specified locality are rejected. For Supervisor/CDPO posts, the same district residency rule generally applies.

Forest Department Posts List

Forest departments work with a clear hierarchy from senior Indian Forest Service officers to field-level staff like Forest Guard and Forest Watcher. Understanding these posts helps candidates know growth opportunities and career progression in forest services.

  • Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (PCCF) / Head of Forest Force
  • Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (APCCF) / Additional Chief Conservator of Forests
  • Chief Conservator of Forests (CCF)
  • Conservator of Forests (CF)
  • Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF) / Divisional Forest Officer (DFO)
  • Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF)
  • Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Range Forest Officer (RFO)
  • Deputy Forest Ranger / Forest Inspector
  • Section Forest Officer / Forester / Van Daroga
  • Beat Forest Officer / Beat Guard / Forester Grade I
  • Forest Guard / Van Rakshak
  • Forest Watcher / Forest Worker / Plantation Worker
  • Forest Ranger (in states where used separately)
  • Field Guard

Most forest department recruitment notifications on this page focus on Forest Guard, Forester, Forest Range Officer, Field Guard and similar Group B, C and D posts.

Eligibility for Forest Department Jobs

Eligibility for forest department jobs depends on the post, state and recruitment authority. Below is a general idea of qualification and age for popular posts like Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer. Candidates should always check the official notification for detailed eligibility criteria.

Education Qualification for Forest Guard, Forester, Ranger

  • Forest Guard / Field Guard / Forest Watcher / Beat Guard: Usually 10th pass or 12th pass from a recognized board, knowledge of local language, and basic physical standards as per state rules.
  • Forester / Section Forest Officer / Deputy Forest Ranger: Generally 12th pass or graduate in any stream or science-related subjects, with physical standards and sometimes driving or technical skills depending on the post.
  • Forest Range Officer (RFO) / Forest Ranger / ACF: Normally graduate degree in Science, Agriculture, Forestry, Environmental Science, Engineering or related disciplines, with higher physical standards and sometimes a competitive exam similar to state PCS level.
  • IFS / Indian Forest Service and senior posts (DCF, CF, CCF, PCCF): Filled through UPSC Indian Forest Service Examination and promotions, requiring a graduate degree and all-India competitive selection.

Age Limit & Relaxation

  • Forest Guard / Forester / Field Guard: Minimum age is usually 18 years and maximum age is around 27–30 years, with age relaxation for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, Ex-servicemen and other categories as per government rules.
  • Forest Range Officer / ACF and higher competitive posts: Age limit often ranges from 21 to 32 years (varies by recruitment), with relaxations for reserved categories.
  • Exact age limit, cut-off date and relaxation details always depend on the particular notification, so candidates should verify from the official advertisement before applying.

Forest Department Jobs Salary Overview

Forest department salary depends on the post, pay level, state and allowances. Senior officers under Indian Forest Service receive higher pay levels, while Forest Guard, Forester and similar posts fall under state government pay matrices.

Selection Process & Exam Pattern

The selection process for Forest Department Jobs 2026 typically includes written examinations, physical tests and document verification. Some posts also conduct medical examination and walking tests due to the demanding nature of field duties.

Written Exam, PET/PST & Document Verification

  • Written Exam: Most Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer recruitments start with a written exam based on objective-type questions (MCQs).
  • PET / PST (Physical Efficiency Test / Physical Standard Test): Candidates who qualify written exam are called for running, walking test, height, chest measurement and other physical parameters as per post and gender.
  • Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates must produce original certificates of age, qualification, caste, residence, sports/NCC and other documents mentioned in the notification.
  • Medical Examination: Many forest posts require medical fitness to ensure candidates can perform field duties in forests, wildlife areas and difficult terrain.

Syllabus Highlights for Forest Guard & Ranger

Typical syllabus for written exam may include:

  • General Knowledge & Current Affairs (national, state-level, environment and forest-related topics)
  • General Hindi / English or regional language (grammar, comprehension, vocabulary)
  • General Mathematics / Quantitative Aptitude (up to 10th or 12th level)
  • General Science and Environmental Studies (forest, wildlife, biodiversity, conservation, climate, basic biology)
  • Reasoning Ability (logical and analytical reasoning)

Exact subject-wise marks, number of questions, negative marking and exam duration will be given in the respective forest department notification.

How to Apply for Forest Department Jobs 2026

Most Forest Department Jobs 2026 applications are accepted either online through official websites or offline via prescribed application forms. Candidates should carefully read the official notification before filling the form.

Online Application Process

  1. Visit the official website of the recruiting body (State Forest Department, State PSC, Staff Selection Board or other government portal).
  2. Find the latest link for Forest Guard / Forester / Forest Range Officer / other forest posts and open the detailed advertisement.
  3. Complete the registration process with basic details like name, mobile number, email ID and verify using OTP if required.
  4. Fill the online application form with personal, educational, address and category information exactly as per documents.
  5. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature and other certificates in the prescribed format and size.
  6. Pay the application fee online through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card or UPI as per your convenience.
  7. Finally, submit the form and take a printout or PDF of the submitted application and payment receipt for future reference.

Documents Required & Application Fee

  • Recent passport size photograph and signature (scanned)
  • Class 10th and 12th mark sheets and certificates
  • Degree / Diploma certificates for graduate-level posts
  • Caste / Category certificate (SC, ST, OBC, EWS, etc.)
  • Domicile / Residence certificate if required by the state
  • NCC, Sports, Ex-serviceman and other quota certificates if applicable
  • Valid ID proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN, Voter ID, Driving Licence, Passport, etc.)

Why Choose Forest Department Jobs?

Forest department jobs are popular among candidates who love nature, wildlife and field work. These jobs offer job security, government benefits and a chance to work in forest conservation, wildlife protection, eco-tourism and community development.

  • Stable government job with regular salary, pension/NPS and allowances
  • Opportunity to work in forests, wildlife sanctuaries, national parks and protected areas
  • Contribution to environment protection, afforestation and biodiversity conservation
  • Good promotion avenues from Forest Guard to Forester, Forest Range Officer and higher posts in many states.

Call letters for online examination will be communicated via email from [email protected]. No hard copy of the call letter will be sent by post. For queries: [email protected] or [email protected].

Apply Online at: https://beep.bfsissc.com/candidate_register/mkt

Important Instructions for Union Bank of India Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Engagement as Apprentice is purely for training and does not guarantee any employment in the Bank.
  • Candidates who have already undergone or are undergoing Apprenticeship training are not eligible.
  • Candidates with more than one year job experience after graduation are not eligible.
  • Only one application should be submitted. Multiple applications will lead to rejection.
  • Proficiency in the local language of the applied state is mandatory.
  • Fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.

The latest Mahanadi Coalfields Limited (MCL) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Asst. Foreman Electrician (Trainee) and Technician (Electrician) (Trainee) posts! Find all details about the MCL Assistant Foreman, Technician new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

FOLLOW US

Mahanadi Coalfields Limited (MCL), a subsidiary of Coal India Limited, has officially released Employment Notice Ref. No. MCL/HQ/Recruitment/Open/2026/311 dated 16.04.2026 for recruitment to 500 posts of Assistant Foreman (Electrical) (Trainee) and Technician (Electrician) (Trainee). MCL Assistant Foreman, Technician Recruitment 2026 Exam Pattern

The selection methodology will comprise of Computer Based Test (CBT) only. No personal interview will be held. The CBT will consist of objective type questions with multiple choice answers. There will be no negative marking for wrong answers. Instructions will be bilingual (English/Hindi).

The CBT will consist of two sections totalling 100 marks: Section "A" comprising 20 questions on General Awareness/Aptitude (1 mark each), and Section "B" comprising 80 questions on Technical Knowledge (1 mark each). Total time: 2 hours.

Section Subject No. of Questions Marks
Section A General Awareness / Aptitude 20 20
Section B Technical Knowledge 80 80
Total   100 100

Qualifying Marks in CBT:

Category Qualifying Marks
General/EWS Candidates 45 marks (45% of 100)
OBC (NCL) Candidates 40 marks (40% of 100)
SC/ST Candidates 35 marks (35% of 100)

Candidates may opt for three Test Cities from the list as per their preference at the time of applying. The same cannot be changed after the online form is submitted. MCL reserves the right to allocate or change the Test City.

MCL Assistant Foreman, Technician Recruitment 2026 Selection Process

The selection for MCL Assistant Foreman, Technician Recruitment 2026 will be based on the following stages:

Computer Based Test (CBT): Eligible candidates will be provisionally called for CBT on the basis of their self-declaration regarding eligibility. Scrutiny/verification of eligibility claims will be done post-declaration of CBT results for candidates called for document verification.

Document Verification: Based on the merit order of marks secured in CBT, shortlisted qualified candidates in the ratio of 1:3 will be called for document verification. In case of a tie in marks, it will be resolved on the basis of Date of Birth (elder candidate first) and thereafter Name alphabets. Candidates must appear with all original certificates/documents and photocopies as per eligibility criteria. Candidates working in Govt./Semi Govt./Autonomous Bodies/PSUs/CIL & Subsidiaries must produce a "No Objection Certificate" from their employer.

Provisional Select List: After verification of documents/certificates, a Provisional Select List of successful candidates will be published on the official website of MCL (https://mahanadicoal.in).

Final Appointment: Provisionally selected candidates will be issued an offer of appointment based on their position in the merit list and with due consideration of reservation policy of Govt. of India for SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/PwBD/EWS/ESM. The validity of the merit panel will be for 01 year or as per extant Govt. guidelines.

Medical Fitness: Selected candidates will have to undergo Initial Medical Examination (IME) in the designated Hospital by the Company's Doctor. Final appointment/joining will be subject to being declared medically fit as per rule. Minimum required height of the candidate should be 60 inches (excluding for the post of Asst. Foreman (Electrical) (Trainee), T&S Gr-C only in case of Dwarfism).

MCL Assistant Foreman, Technician Recruitment 2026 Career Growth

  • For the post of Asst. Foreman (Electrical) (E&M), further career growth of candidates to T&S Gr-B and onwards will be subject to acquisition of Electrical Supervisorship Certificate of Competency valid for Mines as per the Cadre Scheme.
  • For the post of Technician (Electrician) (E&M), trainees in Cat-III will be regularized as Electrician, Cat-IV after successful completion of one year training in E&M cadre and possession of valid minimum requisite Electrician Statutory permit/certificate.

How to Apply for MCL Assistant Foreman, Technician Recruitment 2026

Eligible candidates must submit their application online through the official website mahanadicoal.in under CAREERS >>>> Recruitment section. No other means/mode of application will be accepted.

Step 1 - Read Instructions: Read Important Instructions carefully and click on (√) "I Agree" button.

Step 2 - Register: Register by filling necessary details (Post opted, name, Mobile No. and email ID) and click on Submit Button. Note the Application Sequence No., User ID and Password received on your email and mobile number.

Step 3 - Login & Fill Application: Re-login using User ID and Password. Click on the Application Form option, fill all necessary details in the respective fields and upload required documents, Photo and Signature.

Step 4 - Preview & Submit: Check Preview of the Application Form and make corrections if any. Press the Submit Button.

Step 5 - Pay Fee & Print: Make online payment through Debit Card/Credit Card/Net Banking (as applicable). Take a printout of the application form for future record.

Note: The last date for submission of application is 28/May/2026 up to 11:50 PM. Candidates are advised not to wait till the last date and time for applying online. MCL shall not be responsible if candidates are unable to submit applications at the last hour due to network congestion.

MCL Assistant Foreman, Technician Recruitment 2026 Documents Required

Keep the following scanned documents ready (valid as on cut-off date) before filling the online application:

  • E-Mail ID and Mobile Number
  • Personal and Educational Qualification Details
  • Scanned Copy of Matriculation (X) Certificate/Mark sheets/ITI/Diploma/Degree Certificate/Mark sheets (Jpg/Jpeg/PDF format)
  • Scanned Copy of Valid Caste Certificate (OBC (NCL)/SC/ST) and Income Certificates for EWS (if applicable)
  • Scanned Copy of Ex-Servicemen Certificates (if applicable)
  • Scanned Copy of PwBD Certificates (if applicable)
  • Scanned Copy of LT Permit under IE rules for Mines or 440-550 Volts mining parts permit issued by Competent Authority (if applicable)
  • Scanned Copy of Minimum 01 Year Training/On-the-Job Practical Experience Certificate (if applicable)
  • Details of Debit Card/Credit Card/Net Banking for Fee Payment
  • Scanned Copy of Recent Photograph (not more than 3 weeks old, jpg/jpeg format)
  • Scanned Copy of Signature with Black Ink Pen (jpg/jpeg format)

Shortlisting Based on GATE-2025 Score/Marks in Mining Engineering (Paper Code: MN); shortlisted category-wise in ratio of 1:3 (3 candidates per vacancy) in order of merit
Documents Verification (DV) Shortlisted candidates will be called for Documents Verification; intimated via CIL website and registered email ID only
Initial Medical Examination (IME) By the company's Medical Committee as per CIL Medical Attendance Rules; Medical Committee decision is final and binding
Offer of Appointment Issued only after successful completion of DV & IME

Tie-Breaking Rules

1st Tie-Break Candidate scoring higher percentage marks / CGPA in the minimum eligible qualification (Mining Engineering degree) will be placed higher in the merit list
2nd Tie-Break Seniority of age (older candidate preferred) if tie persists after 1st tie-break

Post-Selection Details

Training Period 1-year training-cum-probation period; on successful completion, service confirmed in E-1 Grade as Officer (Mining)
Posting Anywhere in India including Subsidiary companies and Coalfield areas; candidates must submit 3 posting preferences but final posting is as per CIL Management decision
Service Agreement Bond Minimum service of 60 months from date of joining; Bond of Rs. 3 Lakhs + applicable GST; Rs. 5,000/- per month deducted from salary towards bond money, refunded without interest after 60 months

How to Apply for CIL Management Trainee Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the CIL official website www.coalindia.in under the section "Career with CIL >>> Jobs at Coal India." Hard copy applications will not be accepted under any circumstances. The online portal will be active from 08-05-2026 (10:00 AM) to 07-06-2026 (6:00 PM). Candidates are advised not to wait until the last date and to register and submit their application well within time. No modifications are allowed after the online application form is submitted. If more than one application is received from a candidate, the most recent/current application will be considered as final.

All documents must be uploaded as self-attested scanned copies - scanned documents that are not clear and legible may result in rejection of candidature. Candidates must have an active email ID and mobile number valid for at least one year - no change in email ID or mobile number will be allowed after submission. For queries, candidates may refer to the FAQs on CIL's website or write to [email protected].

Documents to be Uploaded at the Time of Online Application:

  • Recent passport size colour photograph (not older than 3 weeks, JPG/JPEG format)
  • Signature with black ink pen (JPG/JPEG format)
  • Matriculation/Secondary Board Certificate/Admit Card/Birth Certificate (age proof)
  • Mark sheet of graduation (percentage proof); CGPA/GPA/CPI conversion certificate issued by University/Institute (if applicable)
  • Final/Provisional Degree/Graduation Certificate
  • OBC (NCL) / SC / ST Caste Certificate in prescribed format issued on or after 01.04.2026 (if applicable)
  • Income & Asset Certificate (EWS) issued by competent authority based on income for 2025-26, valid for 2026-27 (if applicable)
  • Discharge/Service Certificate (Ex-Servicemen candidates)
  • J&K Domicile Certificate in prescribed format issued by competent authority (if applicable)

Government/PSU/Autonomous body employees must submit NOC from their current employer at the time of DV & IME, or a proper relieving order at the time of joining.

Apply Online at: https://www.coalindia.in (Career with CIL > Jobs at Coal India)

Important Instructions for CIL Management Trainee Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Only Indian Nationals with a valid GATE-2025 score in Mining Engineering (Paper Code: MN) are eligible to apply.
  • Qualifying degree must be regular full-time from a University/Institute recognized by appropriate statutory authority in India.
  • No hard copy applications will be accepted - online submission only via CIL website.
  • Application fee is non-refundable; applications submitted by unqualified candidates will be rejected.
  • No modifications are allowed after online application is submitted - discrepancies between online data and original documents may lead to rejection.
  • GATE scores of any year other than GATE-2025 are not valid and will not be considered.
  • OBC (Creamy Layer) candidates must apply under General (UR) category and are not entitled to OBC (NCL) concession/reservation.
  • OBC (NCL) caste certificate must be issued on or after 01.04.2026 in the latest prescribed format for Central Government/PSU appointment purpose.
  • Government/PSU employees must submit NOC/relieving letter from current employer at DV & IME stage or at the time of joining - no pay protection or bond transfer benefits on joining.
  • Selected candidates must execute a service agreement bond of Rs. 3 Lakhs + GST and serve for a minimum of 60 months from date of joining.
  • Candidates must keep their registered email ID and mobile number active for at least one year - no changes will be allowed.
  • Canvassing in any form will disqualify the candidature.
  • Any modifications/amendments/corrigendum in the advertisement will be published only on CIL's website www.coalindia.in.
  • All disputes related to this recruitment will be settled within the jurisdiction of Kolkata only.
  • CIL reserves the right to cancel/restrict/modify/alter the recruitment process at any stage without assigning any reason.
  • The decision of the Chairman, CIL on all matters relating to eligibility, acceptance/rejection of applications, selection, and posting is final and binding.

Check complete details for HPSCB Junior Clerk Online Form 2026. Includes important dates, application link, eligibility, syllabus and result info — updated live on OnlyJobs.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The Himachal Pradesh State Co-operative Bank Ltd. (HPSCB) has officially announced the Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 with 91 openings. The detailed notification was published on 09 May 2026 at www.hpscb.bank.in. Candidates who meet the age criteria of 18–45 years (with relaxations for reserved categories) and hold a Graduate degree in any discipline along with the required cooperative society experience can now submit their online applications from 09 May 2026 to 29 May 2026. Direct links to download the notification and apply online are shared below

FOLLOW US

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

The Himachal Pradesh State Co-operative Bank Ltd. (HPSCB), Head Office - The Mall, Shimla (H.P.) - 171001 has officially released the detailed notification for recruitment to 91 posts of Junior Clerks (quota for PACS/OCS of HP) on Regular Basis through IBPS, Mumbai. The notification includes complete details on vacancies, eligibility, selection process, examination pattern, syllabus, PwBD guidelines, fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to download and read the full notification before applying online. The notification PDF is available on the official HPSCB website.

  • Download HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organisation Name The Himachal Pradesh State Co-operative Bank Ltd. (HPSCB), Shimla - 171001
Post Name Junior Clerk (quota for PACS/OCS of HP)
Conducting Body IBPS, Mumbai (Online Examination conducted through IBPS)
Quota Type PACS Quota (Trained Secretaries of Primary Agricultural Cooperative Societies of HP) and OCS Quota (Employees of Other Cooperative Societies and PACS employees not covered as Trained Secretaries)
Total Vacancies 91 (PACS Quota: 74 + OCS Quota: 17)
Pay Scale Minimum Pay of Rs. 19,900/- (Level-2) + admissible allowances per month (Regular Basis)
Application Mode Online only (via HPSCB website - www.hpscb.bank.in)
Selection Mode Online Examination (150 marks, 150 minutes) + Document Verification/Scrutiny (No Personal Interview)
Mandatory Requirement Bonafide Himachali for all reserved categories; valid experience certificate from cooperative society (PACS: min. 3 years; OCS: min. 5 years) countersigned by Assistant Registrar, Cooperative Societies of H.P.
Official Website https://www.hpscb.bank.in

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Event Date
Opening Date for Submission of Online Application 09-05-2026
Last Date for Submission of Online Application 29-05-2026
Application Fee Payment Window 09-05-2026 to 29-05-2026 (Online Mode Only)
Date of Downloading of Call Letters Around 7 days before examination date
Date of Online Examination Expected 5-6 weeks after last date of submission (exact date to be notified on Bank's website)
Date of Declaration of Result for Online Examination Around 15 days after conduct of examination
Age Calculated As On 01-01-2026

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 91 vacancies have been announced for the post of Junior Clerk under two quota categories - PACS Quota (74 posts) and OCS Quota (17 posts). These are distributed across General (UR), EWS, SC, ST, OBC, Ex-Servicemen, IRDP, WFF, and PwBD categories. All reserved category benefits apply only to Bonafide Himachali candidates. Number of posts may vary depending on actual requirement at the time of final selection.

PACS Quota - Category-wise Vacancy (Quota reserved for Trained Secretaries of PACS of HP)

Category No. of Vacancies
GEN / UR 19
EWS of HP 07
SC of HP 12
ST of HP 02
OBC of HP 11
GEN (Ex-Servicemen) of HP 08
SC (Ex-Servicemen) of HP 03
ST (Ex-Servicemen) of HP 01
OBC (Ex-Servicemen) of HP 02
SC-IRDP of HP 02
OBC-IRDP of HP 02
GEN (WFF) of HP 01
SC (WFF) of HP 01
PwBDs of HP (01 each for: Blindness & Low Vision; Deaf & Hard of Hearing; Locomotor Disability including Cerebral Palsy, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Acid Attack Victims and Muscular Dystrophy) 03
Sub-Total (PACS Quota) 74

OCS Quota - Category-wise Vacancy (Quota reserved for Employees of Other Cooperative Societies and PACS employees not covered as Trained Secretaries)

Category No. of Vacancies
GEN / UR 06
EWS of HP 02
SC of HP 02
ST of HP 01
OBC of HP 02
OBC-IRDP of HP 01
SC-IRDP of HP 01
GEN (Ex-Servicemen) of HP 01
SC (Ex-Servicemen) of HP 01
Sub-Total (OCS Quota) 17

Grand Total: 91 Posts (PACS: 74 + OCS: 17)

PH (Physically Handicapped / PwBD) Sub-Category Vacancy (Total 03 Posts - under PACS Quota only)

Sl. No. Sub-Category of PwBD Post No. of Posts
1 Blindness and Low Vision 01
2 Deaf and Hard of Hearing 01
3 Locomotor Disability including Cerebral Palsy, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Acid Attack Victims and Muscular Dystrophy 01

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on 01-01-2026. All reserved category candidates must be Bonafide Himachalis. Reservation benefits are applicable only to Bonafide Himachali candidates. OBC candidates must submit a valid Non-Creamy Layer certificate from competent authority. Candidates belonging to SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBDs/Ex-SM and other reserved categories of HP may apply against unreserved/general category posts but will not be eligible for age relaxation or fee concession in such cases. Candidates must also ensure they are not directly or indirectly a defaulter of their society.

Experience certificates must be issued by the concerned Inspector of Society and countersigned by the Assistant Registrar, Cooperative Societies of H.P. with authentic certification. Such certificate must be supported by: (i) Copy of resolution of the managing committee for initial appointment, and (ii) Copy of relevant pages of statutory audit report/note for prescribed years showing payment of wages/salary from the society account.

Educational Qualification

Post / Quota Essential Qualifications
Junior Clerk (PACS Quota - Trained Secretaries of PACS of HP) (i) Graduate in any discipline from a recognized University (Candidates must have passed Matriculation and 10+2 from a School/Institution within Himachal Pradesh - not applicable for Bonafide Himachalis); (ii) Minimum 3 years continuous service as Secretary as defined in Rule 2(vii) of H.P. Cooperative Societies Rules, 1971 of Primary Agriculture Credit Society; (iii) All employees of PACS covered under definition of "Trained Secretaries" are eligible; (iv) Candidate should not be a defaulter of his/her society
Junior Clerk (OCS Quota - Employees of Other Cooperative Societies and PACS employees not covered as Trained Secretaries) (i) Graduate in any discipline from a recognized University (Matriculation and 10+2 from HP school - not applicable for Bonafide Himachalis); (ii) Minimum 5 years continuous service in a cooperative society; (iii) All employees of other cooperative societies and PACS employees not covered under definition of "Trained Secretaries" are eligible; (iv) Candidate should not be a defaulter of his/her society

Types of PACS and OCS covered under this recruitment:

Quota Type Societies Covered
PACS (Primary Agricultural Credit Societies) Cooperative Multipurpose Societies Ltd.; Gram Service Cooperative Societies Ltd.
OCS (Other Cooperative Societies) Fruit Growers and Marketing Society Ltd.; Marketing and Consumer Cooperative Societies Ltd.; Cooperative Consumer Store; Thrift & Credit Society Ltd.; All other Cooperative Societies registered under HP Cooperative Societies Act, 1968

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

Candidates must be above 18 years and below 45 years of age as on 01-01-2026. Candidates born between 02-01-1981 and 01-01-2008 (both days inclusive) are eligible. Age relaxation is applicable only to Bonafide Himachali candidates of the respective reserved categories. Selected PwBD candidates will have to submit a Medical Certificate from a competent authority confirming physical fitness to perform duties.

Category Age Relaxation
General (UR) 18 to 45 years (as on 01-01-2026)
SC of HP 5 years relaxation (max 50 years)
ST of HP 5 years relaxation (max 50 years)
OBC of HP 5 years relaxation (max 50 years)
EWS of HP As per HP Govt. norms
Physically Handicapped (PwBD) 5 years relaxation (minimum 40% disability required)
Ward of Freedom Fighter (WFF) of HP 5 years relaxation
Ex-Servicemen (Reserved Vacancies) Age at time of joining military service should not exceed upper age limit; present age must be less than 58 years (Maximum relaxation: 12 years)
Ex-Servicemen (Unreserved Vacancies) Actual age minus approved military service must not exceed prescribed maximum age by more than 3 years (Maximum relaxation: 12 years)

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

Candidates applying for the post of Junior Clerk are required to pay a non-refundable application fee as specified below. The fee must be paid online through the HPSCB application portal. Fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances. Bank transaction charges for online payment will have to be borne by the candidate.

Category Application Fee
General (UR) and OBC of H.P. Rs. 1,000/- (Rupees One Thousand only) - Non-refundable
EWS of H.P. (Economically Weaker Sections) Rs. 800/- (Rupees Eight Hundred only) - Non-refundable
SC / ST / IRDP / BPL / ANTODAYA of HP Rs. 800/- (Rupees Eight Hundred only) - Non-refundable
Female Candidates of All Categories of HP Rs. 800/- (Rupees Eight Hundred only) - Non-refundable

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 consists of 2 stages - Online Examination (150 marks, 150 minutes, conducted through IBPS Mumbai) followed by Document Verification/Scrutiny. There is no personal interview. Candidates up to 1.75 times of vacant posts will be called for document verification. Final selection will be made strictly on the basis of merit in the online examination after document verification. Candidates must score minimum 40% marks in the online examination to be shortlisted for document verification. Candidates declared successful who do not personally attend document verification will have their candidature cancelled.

Stage 1 - Online Examination (150 Marks, 150 Minutes)

Sl. No. Subject No. of Questions Marks Duration
1 Reasoning 30 30 150 Minutes (2½ Hours) - No separate time limit per section
2 Numerical Ability 30 30
3 English 30 30
4 General Awareness (including HP General Knowledge) 30 30
5 H.P. Cooperative Societies Act and H.P. Cooperative Societies Rules 30 30
Total 150 150  

Stage 2 - Document Verification / Scrutiny

Shortlisting Ratio Candidates up to 1.75 times of vacant posts will be called for document verification
Minimum Qualifying Marks 40% of total marks (i.e., minimum 60 out of 150) in online examination
No Negative Marking There is NO negative marking in the online examination
Question Paper Type Objective type multiple choice (MCQ) - bilingual (English and Hindi)

Tie-Breaking Rules

1st Tie-Break Preference to candidates possessing higher academic qualifications
2nd Tie-Break Preference to candidates belonging to the State of H.P.

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Exam Syllabus

The Online Examination consists of 5 sections - Reasoning, Numerical Ability, English, General Awareness, and H.P. Cooperative Societies Act and Rules - each carrying 30 marks (total 150 marks, 150 minutes). The level of examination and syllabus is in accordance with the minimum educational qualification criteria i.e. Graduation level. The question paper is bilingual (English and Hindi) and is of objective type MCQ. There is no negative marking. Preference will be given to candidates possessing knowledge of customs, traditions, languages, and culture of Himachal Pradesh and the test shall include questions on such subjects.

Section Topic Key Coverage Marks
Section 1 Reasoning Logical Reasoning, Analytical Reasoning, Verbal and Non-Verbal Reasoning 30
Section 2 Numerical Ability Quantitative Aptitude, Number Systems, Arithmetic, Data Interpretation 30
Section 3 English Reading Comprehension, Grammar, Vocabulary, Sentence Completion 30
Section 4 General Awareness (including HP GK) General Awareness, Current Affairs, HP General Knowledge, Customs, Traditions, Languages and Culture of Himachal Pradesh 30
Section 5 H.P. Cooperative Societies Act & H.P. Cooperative Societies Rules Provisions of HP Cooperative Societies Act and HP Cooperative Societies Rules 30

How to Apply for HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026

Applications must be submitted online only through the Bank's website www.hpscb.bank.in by clicking the "APPLY ONLINE" option. Hard copy applications or applications by any other mode will not be accepted. The online application window is open from 09-05-2026 to 29-05-2026. Candidates must register on the portal, make fee payment online, and upload all required documents. No change will be possible after clicking the "COMPLETE REGISTRATION" button. Candidates are advised to keep their valid personal email ID and mobile number active till the completion of the entire recruitment process.

Before applying, candidates must have ready: scanned photograph (4.5 cm × 3.5 cm), signature (with black ink on white paper), left thumb impression (on white paper with black or blue ink), and a hand-written declaration in English on white paper with black ink. Signature in CAPITAL LETTERS will NOT be accepted. The hand-written declaration text is: "I, _______ (Name of the candidate), hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required."

Documents Required to be Produced at Document Verification (when called):

  • Graduation degree certificate and mark sheets from recognized University
  • Matriculation and 10+2 certificates from HP school/institution (if applicable)
  • Experience Certificate countersigned by Assistant Registrar, Cooperative Societies of H.P. - with copy of managing committee resolution for initial appointment and statutory audit report pages (3 years for PACS quota; 5 years for OCS quota)
  • Age proof (10th/12th class mark sheet/certificate)
  • Bonafide Himachali Certificate from competent authority (for reserved category candidates)
  • SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD/WFF/IRDP certificate from competent authority (if applicable)
  • Non-Creamy Layer (NCL) Certificate (for OBC candidates) - in latest prescribed format
  • EWS Income and Asset Certificate issued by Deputy Commissioner/SDO (Civil)/Tehsildar (for EWS candidates) - family income below Rs. 4 Lakhs per annum
  • PwBD Certificate (minimum 40% disability) + Medical Certificate from competent authority confirming fitness for duty (for PwBD candidates)
  • Discharge Certificate (for Ex-Servicemen candidates)
  • WFF Certificate (for Ward of Freedom Fighter candidates)
  • IRDP/BPL/Antodaya Certificate countersigned by Block Development Officer (if applicable)
  • NOC and Good Character Certificate from current employer (for candidates in regular/confirmed service)
  • e-Receipt and printout of online application form

Apply Online at: https://www.hpscb.bank.in

HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Application Link and Form Details

The online application for HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 is to be filled and submitted through the Bank's official website. The step-by-step process is as follows:

Step Action Details
Step 1 Visit Bank's Website Go to www.hpscb.bank.in → Click "APPLY ONLINE" → Choose "Click here for New Registration" → Enter Name, Contact Details, and Email ID → Note the Provisional Registration Number and Password generated
Step 2 Fill the Application Form Fill all personal details, category, qualification, and experience details. Use "SAVE AND NEXT" to save data if unable to complete in one go. Details must match exactly as in certificates/mark sheets.
Step 3 Upload Documents Upload: (1) Photograph (4.5×3.5 cm, 20-50 KB, JPG); (2) Signature (black ink, 10-20 KB, JPG); (3) Left Thumb Impression (black/blue ink on white paper, 20-50 KB, JPG); (4) Hand-written Declaration (black ink on white paper, English only, 50-100 KB, JPG). Capture live webcam photo as required.
Step 4 Preview and Complete Registration Click "Preview Tab" to verify all details → Modify if required → Click "COMPLETE REGISTRATION" only after verifying everything is correct. No changes allowed after this step.
Step 5 Pay Application Fee Click "Payment" Tab → Pay online via Debit Card (RuPay/Visa/MasterCard/Maestro), Credit Card, Internet Banking, IMPS, or Mobile Wallets → Wait for server intimation (do not press Back/Refresh) → Download e-Receipt
Step 6 Submit and Print Click "Submit" → Take printout of e-Receipt and online application form with fee details → Download Call Letter (around 7 days before exam) from www.hpscb.bank.in using Registration Number/Roll Number and Password/Date of Birth

For queries: [email protected]

Apply Online at: https://www.hpscb.bank.in

Important Instructions for HPSCB Junior Clerk Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • All reserved category benefits including age relaxation and fee concession apply only to Bonafide Himachali candidates.
  • Candidates must possess valid experience certificate from their cooperative society - false experience certificates will result in summary cancellation of candidature irrespective of qualifying the online examination.
  • Experience certificates must be issued by the concerned Inspector of Society and countersigned by the Assistant Registrar, Cooperative Societies of H.P. supported by managing committee resolution and statutory audit report pages.
  • No hard copy applications will be accepted - online submission only via www.hpscb.bank.in.
  • Application fee is non-refundable; fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.
  • Signature in CAPITAL LETTERS and hand-written declaration not in candidate's own handwriting or not in English will be treated as invalid.
  • Candidates already in regular (confirmed) service must submit NOC and good character certificate from current employer at document verification - absence of NOC will result in cancellation of candidature.
  • There is NO negative marking in the online examination; minimum 40% marks required to qualify.
  • Candidates must appear in person at document verification - non-appearance will lead to cancellation of candidature without notice.
  • OBC candidates must submit a valid Non-Creamy Layer certificate; EWS candidates must have family gross annual income below Rs. 4 Lakhs per annum.
  • PwBD candidates must have minimum 40% degree of disability and must submit Medical Certificate from competent authority confirming fitness for duty at selected post.
  • Wards of Ex-Servicemen are NOT eligible for posts advertised for Ex-Servicemen.
  • Selected candidates will be on probation for a period of two years (extendable up to maximum 3 years).
  • No TA/DA is admissible for appearing in the online examination.
  • Selected candidates will be posted anywhere in the area of operation of H.P. State Cooperative Bank in Himachal Pradesh.
  • For any dispute related to selection process, the decision of the Registrar, Co-operative Societies of Himachal Pradesh shall be binding and final; jurisdiction shall be Shimla (HP) only.
  • Candidates are advised to visit www.hpscb.bank.in regularly for all updates regarding the recruitment process.

South Indian Bank Junior Officer (Operations) Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

The South Indian Bank Limited, a Premier Scheduled Commercial Bank in India, has officially released a recruitment notification for the post of Junior Officer (Operations) for the Hyderabad and Vijayawada Regions covering the states of Andhra Pradesh and Telangana. The notification includes complete details on eligibility, terms of employment, salary/compensation, mode of selection, application fee, and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification carefully before applying online. The notification is available on the official South Indian Bank recruitment website.

  • Download South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

South Indian Bank Junior Officer (Operations) Recruitment 2026 Overview

Organization Name The South Indian Bank Ltd.
Post Name Junior Officer (Operations)
Recruitment Region Hyderabad and Vijayawada Regions (States of Andhra Pradesh & Telangana)
Type of Employment Contract - initially for 3 years, renewable at Bank's sole discretion based on performance
Career Progression High performing candidates may be considered for regular employment as Assistant Manager (Scale I) after contract period

Application Fee NIL

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

Online Application - End Date 13-05-2026
Eligibility Criteria Calculated As On 31-03-2026

Interview Date To be notified on Bank's official website

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on 31.03.2026. Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply. Candidates who have previously attended Group Discussion / Personal Interview for the Junior Officers role as per any previous notification of the Bank and were not included in the final selection list are not eligible to apply for this notification. Proficiency in local language (Telugu) will be given preference. All eligibility is provisional and subject to verification of original documents when a candidate reports for interview/training/joining.

Educational Qualification

Qualification Details
Minimum Qualification Any Graduation with minimum 50% marks from a recognized university.
Stream of Education Regular stream only: SSLC/SSC, HSC and Graduation (10+2+ Graduation under regular stream).
Work Experience Minimum 1 year of experience in Bank / NBFCs.
Special Note Candidates who have attended Group Discussion / Personal Interview for Junior Officers role as per previous notification(s) and were not included in the final selection list are NOT eligible to apply.

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit

The maximum age limit is 28 years as on 31.03.2026. Age relaxation of 5 years is available for SC/ST candidates. Candidates belonging to SC/ST must submit attested copies of certificates from a competent authority to prove their status as and when called for.

Category Age Limit
General (UR) Maximum 28 years (as on 31.03.2026)
SC / ST 5 years of age relaxation (maximum 33 years)

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

No application fee is required for this post. Candidates can apply free of cost through the official South Indian Bank recruitment portal.

Category Recruitment Fee
All Categories NIL (No Application Fee)

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Terms of Employment

Selected candidates will be appointed on a contract basis initially for a period of 3 years, renewable at the sole discretion of the Bank based on performance evaluation. On completion of the initial contract period or any extension, high performing candidates may, without any obligation and in the Bank's sole and absolute discretion, be considered for regular employment as Assistant Manager (Scale I), subject to the then prevailing norms of the Bank. There will be no mandatory minimum service period as stipulated in the case of staff members recruited under regular employment.

Parameter Details
Designation / Job Role Junior Officer (Operations)
Term of Appointment Contract - initially 3 years, renewable at Bank's sole discretion based on performance
Career Progression High performers may be considered for regular employment as Assistant Manager (Scale I) after contract period, at Bank's sole discretion
Posting Location Anywhere in Andhra Pradesh & Telangana as per Bank's requirement; liable to be transferred anywhere in India at Bank's sole discretion
Total CTC Rs. 4.86 lakhs to Rs. 5.04 lakhs per annum (inclusive of NPS contribution, Insurance Premium and Variable Pay based on performance)
Official Duty Allowance Eligible Lodging, Halting and Travelling allowance for official travel, as per Bank norms
Insurance Group Medical Insurance and Group Accident Insurance at the cost of the Bank; Group Life Insurance with premium borne by the employee
Increment Annual increment during contract period based on performance evaluation or other parameters as decided by the Bank

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

The selection process for South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 consists of an Online Test and/or Group Discussion, followed by a Preliminary and/or Final Interview. The Online Test and Group Discussion are elimination stages - only test-qualified candidates will be called for Group Discussion or Personal Interview, and only candidates shortlisted from the Group Discussion will be advanced to the Personal Interview. The Bank reserves the right to modify the selection process based on the number of applications received. Mere eligibility will not vest any right on the applicant to be called for the Recruitment Process. All marks/scores/grades at any stage shall be treated as strictly confidential.

Stage Details Nature
Stage 1 Online Test (if conducted) Elimination Stage - conducted at locations identified by the Bank
Stage 2 Group Discussion (if conducted) Elimination Stage - only Online Test qualified candidates called
Stage 3 Preliminary and/or Final Personal Interview Only Group Discussion shortlisted candidates advanced

South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 Online Application Form

Eligible candidates who wish to apply for the South Indian Bank Junior Officer (Operations) posts can do so by submitting their application form online. The application process is online only through the official South Indian Bank recruitment portal, and candidates must ensure that they complete all steps before the closing date.

The application form for the South Indian Bank Junior Officer (Operations) Recruitment 2026 is available on the official South Indian Bank recruitment website at https://recruit.southindianbank.bank.in/RDC/. Eligible candidates with any Graduation (minimum 50% marks) and minimum 1 year of experience in Bank/NBFCs are encouraged to fill out the online application form and upload the required documents before the last date.

Online Apply Link for South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026

How to Apply for South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026

Applicants can apply online only through the Bank's official recruitment website at https://recruit.southindianbank.bank.in/RDC/ from 06-05-2026 to 13-05-2026. No other mode of application will be accepted. Before registering, applicants must ensure they have a valid Email ID and mobile number in their name. An applicant can only apply for vacancies in one State - making multiple registrations will result in disqualification.

There is no provision to modify the submitted online application - candidates are requested to fill in the application form with utmost care. After successful submission, a system generated User ID (Application Ref. ID) will be generated - candidates must note this for future reference. An email with the application PDF and login credentials will be sent to the registered email ID upon successful submission. The physical copy of the application need not be sent to the Bank.

Documents / Upload Specifications:

  • Photograph: Recent passport size colour photo, white background, no caps/hats/dark glasses - Resolution: 378 x 437 pixels, maximum 50 KB (JPEG/JPG format)
  • Signature: Signed on white paper with black ink pen - Resolution: 140 x 110 pixels, maximum 50 KB
  • Resume: PDF format, file size not exceeding 1 MB
  • Education Documents: Certificates and marklists of X/SSLC (must include D.O.B), XII/HSC and final marklist and certificate of Graduation - combined as a single PDF file, not exceeding 3 MB

Documents to be Produced When Called for (Originals):

  • Proof of age (SSLC/SSC certificate with Date of Birth)
  • Educational qualification certificates and marklists (X, XII, Graduation)
  • Work experience certificate(s) proving minimum 1 year in Bank/NBFCs
  • SC/ST certificate from competent authority (if applicable)
  • Proof of nationality / domicile as applicable
  • Any other documents as required by the Bank

For queries, contact: Toll Free: 1800-425-1809 / 1800-102-9408, 0484-277-1383 | Email: [email protected]

Apply Online at: https://recruit.southindianbank.bank.in/RDC/

Important Instructions for South Indian Bank Junior Officer Recruitment 2026 Candidates

  • Beware of fraudulent job alerts - apply only through the official online portal. Applications through any other mode will not be accepted.
  • Candidates who have previously attended Group Discussion / Personal Interview for Junior Officers role as per any previous notification and were not included in the final selection list are NOT eligible to apply.
  • An applicant can apply for vacancies in only one State - multiple registrations will lead to disqualification.
  • There is no provision to modify the submitted online application - fill in all details carefully before submission.
  • No application fee is required for this post.
  • Appointment is subject to Medical fitness, satisfactory background verification including CIBIL/credit history check, police clearance, and completion of other formalities as per Bank rules.
  • Canvassing in any form will be a disqualification.
  • Applicants must appear for the Recruitment Process at their own cost.
  • Call letters/advice will be sent by email only - no hard copy will be sent. Candidates are advised to check their registered email and the Bank's official website regularly.
  • All marks/scores/grades obtained at any stage of the selection process are strictly confidential and will not be disclosed to any party including candidates.
  • Applicants are advised to avoid last-minute rush and submit the online application well before the closing date, as website access may be delayed due to heavy internet traffic near the deadline.
  • The Bank reserves the right to make modifications to the selection process and the qualifying criteria at any stage, and such decisions will be final and binding.

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 Overview

Field Details
Commission/Organization Name Reserve Bank of India Services Board (RBISB), Mumbai
Advertisement No. RBISB/DA/01/2026-27
Post Name Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) (On Probation) – General / DEPR / DSIM Cadres
Department Reserve Bank of India (RBI)
Service General Cadre / DEPR Cadre / DSIM Cadre

Phase-II Exam – DEPR / DSIM Cadre 26-07-2026

RBI Grade B Officer Recruitment 2026 - Vacancy Details

A total of 60 vacancies have been announced across three cadres – General, DEPR, and DSIM. Vacancies are distributed across GEN/UR, EWS, OBC, SC, and ST categories.

Post Total
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – General Cadre 40
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – DEPR Cadre 10
Officers in Grade 'B' (DR) – DSIM Cadre 10
Total 60

Sr. No. Post PwBD Category A PwBD Category B PwBD Category C PwBD Category D
1 Grade 'B' (DR) – General – – 1 1
